name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayahs

```arabic
فَذَكِّرْ فَمَآ أَنتَ بِنِعْمَتِ رَبِّكَ بِكَاهِنٍ وَلَا مَجْنُونٍ (٢٩)
```

```english
So admonish. By your Lord’s blessing, you are not a soothsayer, nor mad. (29)
```

```arabic
أَمْ يَقُولُونَ شَاعِرٌ نَّتَرَبَّصُ بِهِۦ رَيْبَ ٱلْمَنُونِ (٣٠)
```

```english
Do they say, ‘[He is] a poet, for whom we await a fatal accident’? (30)
```

```arabic
قُلْ تَرَبَّصُوا۟ فَإِنِّى مَعَكُم مِّنَ ٱلْمُتَرَبِّصِينَ (٣١)
```

```english
Say, ‘Wait! I too am waiting along with you.’ (31)
```

```arabic
أَمْ تَأْمُرُهُمْ أَحْلَـٰمُهُم بِهَـٰذَآ ۚ أَمْ هُمْ قَوْمٌ طَاغُونَ (٣٢)
```

```english
Is it their intellect which prompts them to [say] this, or are they a rebellious lot? (32)
```

```arabic
أَمْ يَقُولُونَ تَقَوَّلَهُۥ ۚ بَل لَّا يُؤْمِنُونَ (٣٣)
```

```english
Do they say, ‘He has improvised it [himself]?’ Rather, they have no faith! (33)
```

```arabic
فَلْيَأْتُوا۟ بِحَدِيثٍ مِّثْلِهِۦٓ إِن كَانُوا۟ صَـٰدِقِينَ (٣٤)
```

```english
Let them bring a discourse like it, if they are truthful. (34)
```

```arabic
أَمْ خُلِقُوا۟ مِنْ غَيْرِ شَىْءٍ أَمْ هُمُ ٱلْخَـٰلِقُونَ (٣٥)
```

```english
Were they created from nothing? Or are they [their own] creators? (35)
```

```arabic
أَمْ خَلَقُوا۟ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ ۚ بَل لَّا يُوقِنُونَ (٣٦)
```

```english
Did they create the heavens and the earth? Rather, they have no certainty! (36)
```

```arabic
أَمْ عِندَهُمْ خَزَآئِنُ رَبِّكَ أَمْ هُمُ ٱلْمُصَۣيْطِرُونَ (٣٧)
```

```english
Do they possess the treasuries of your Lord? Or do they control [them]? (37)
```

```arabic
أَمْ لَهُمْ سُلَّمٌ يَسْتَمِعُونَ فِيهِ ۖ فَلْيَأْتِ مُسْتَمِعُهُم بِسُلْطَـٰنٍ مُّبِينٍ (٣٨)
```

```english
Do they have a ladder [leading up to heaven] whereby they eavesdrop? If so, let their eavesdropper produce a manifest authority. (38)
```

```arabic
أَمْ لَهُ ٱلْبَنَـٰتُ وَلَكُمُ ٱلْبَنُونَ (٣٩)
```

```english
Does He have daughters while you have sons? (39)
```

```arabic
أَمْ تَسْـَٔلُهُمْ أَجْرًا فَهُم مِّن مَّغْرَمٍ مُّثْقَلُونَ (٤٠)
```

```english
Do you ask them for a reward, so that they are [wary of] being weighed down with debt? (40)
```

```arabic
أَمْ عِندَهُمُ ٱلْغَيْبُ فَهُمْ يَكْتُبُونَ (٤١)
```

```english
Do they have [access to] the Unseen, which they write down? (41)
```

```arabic
أَمْ يُرِيدُونَ كَيْدًا ۖ فَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ هُمُ ٱلْمَكِيدُونَ (٤٢)
```

```english
Do they seek to outmaneuver [Allah]? But it is the faithless who are the outmaneuvered ones! (42)
```

```arabic
أَمْ لَهُمْ إِلَـٰهٌ غَيْرُ ٱللَّهِ ۚ سُبْحَـٰنَ ٱللَّهِ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ (٤٣)
```

```english
Do they have any god other than Allah? Clear is Allah of any partners that they may ascribe [to Him]! (43)
```

```arabic
وَإِن يَرَوْا۟ كِسْفًا مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ سَاقِطًا يَقُولُوا۟ سَحَابٌ مَّرْكُومٌ (٤٤)
```

```english
Were they to see a fragment falling from the sky, they would say, ‘A cumulous cloud.’ (44)
```

## (Commentary)

After informing of the punishment that will occur on the Day of Resurrection and that it will surely strike the deniers, while the God-conscious (المتقون) — *those who guard themselves against evil through piety and awareness of Allah* — will be in gardens and bliss, their eyes delighted, He commanded the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) to proceed with his mission and reminder. This indicates that he is fit to establish the true call, and there is no excuse for these deniers in their rejection of him and their rebuffing of his invitation.

Thus, He negated all conceivable excuses for them, which consist of sixteen matters. One half of these pertains to the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله); had any of them been true of him, it would have stripped him of his eligibility to be followed and would have been an obstacle to accepting his word—such as his being a soothsayer, a madman, a poet, or a fabricator (متقول) forging lies against Allah, or his asking for a reward for his call. The other half pertains to the deniers themselves, such as the notion that they were created from nothing, or that they are the creators, or that their intellects command them to deny, among other things. Despite this, the verses are not devoid of severe rebuke for their denial.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **فَذَكِّرْ فَمَا أَنْتَ بِنِعْمَةِ رَبِّكَ بِكَاهِنٍ وَلَا مَجْنُونٍ** **"So proclaim the [divine] message: by your Lord’s blessing you are neither a soothsayer nor mad"** (52:29). This is a derivation from what preceded of the emphasized report regarding the occurrence of the divine punishment on the Day of Resurrection, and that it will envelop the deniers, while the God-conscious will be in protection from it, enjoying the bliss of Paradise.

The verse is in the sense of saying: If this is the truth, then remind, for you only remind and warn with the truth, and you are not, as they charge you, a soothsayer or a madman.

The qualification of the negation with His saying, **بِنِعْمَةِ رَبِّكَ** **"by your Lord’s blessing,"** conveys the sense of bestowing a favor specifically upon the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله). This specific favor is not merely due to the absence of soothsaying and madness—for most people possess this quality—but rather due to his (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) being clothed in the specific blessing belonging to him, which prevents these attributes, such as soothsaying, madness, and others, from befalling him.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **أَمْ يَقُولُونَ شَاعِرٌ نَتَرَبَّصُ بِهِ رَيْبَ الْمَنُونِ** **"Do they say, ‘[He is] a poet; we await for him some calamity of fortune’?"** (52:30). The word **أَمْ** is disjunctive (منقطعة). **التربص** means waiting. In *Majma‘ al-Bayan*, it says: **التربص** is waiting for something to change from one state to its opposite; **المنون** refers to fate and death; and **الريب** is anxiety and agitation. Thus, **ريب المنون** is the agitation of death.

The summary of the meaning is: Rather, they say he—meaning the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله)—is a poet for whom we await death, until he dies, his mention is extinguished, and his trace is forgotten, so we may be relieved of him.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **قُلْ تَرَبَّصُوا فَإِنِّي مَعَكُمْ مِنَ الْمُتَرَبِّصِينَ** **"Say, ‘Wait! I too am waiting with you’"** (52:31). The Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) was commanded to tell them to wait, just as they were satisfied with that for themselves. This is a command of threat; meaning: wait as you see fit for yourselves, for there is a matter whose occurrence ought to be expected, and I am waiting for it just as you are, but it shall be against you, not for you—and that is your destruction and the falling of punishment upon you.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **أَمْ تَأْمُرُهُمْ أَحْلَامُهُمْ بِهَذَا** **"Do their intellects prompt them to [say] this?"** (52:32). **الأحلام** is the plural of **حلم**, which means the intellect (العقل). The word **أَمْ** is disjunctive, and the speech implies an interrogative. The demonstration **بِهَذَا** (to this) refers to what they say to the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) and their awaiting his end.

The meaning is: Rather, do their intellects command them to say what they say and to await his death? For what intellect would repel the truth with such vanities?

**His saying, the Exalted:** **أَمْ هُمْ قَوْمٌ طَاغُونَ** **"Or are they a rebellious people?"** (52:32). That is, their intellects did not command them to do this; rather, they are rebellious (طاغون), and their rebellion (طغيان) is what drove them to this.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **أَمْ يَقُولُونَ تَقَوَّلَهُ بَلْ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ** **"Do they say, ‘He has fabricated it’? Rather, they do not have faith"** (52:33). It is said in *al-Majma‘*: **التقول** is to laboriously devise speech, and it is only used in the context of lying. The meaning is: Rather, they say: He has invented the Quran and attributed it to Allah falsely and as a fabrication. Nay, they do not believe, so they hurl this slander at him.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **فَلْيَأْتُوا بِحَدِيثٍ مِثْلِهِ إِنْ كَانُوا صَادِقِينَ** **"Let them bring a discourse like it, if they are truthful"** (52:34). This is a response to their saying, **تَقَوَّلَهُ** (he fabricated it), on the grounds that if it were the speech of the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله), it would be human speech similar to all other speech, and other speech would be similar to it. Thus, it would be possible for them to bring a discourse like it. So let them bring a discourse like it if they are truthful in their claim of fabrication. Rather, it is divine speech upon which the signs of inimitability (الإعجاز) are manifest, such that humans are incapable of producing its like. The discussion regarding the aspects of the Quran's inimitability was previously detailed in the commentary on Surah al-Baqarah, verse 23.

It is possible to take the verse as a refutation of all that preceded of their reported sayings—that he is a soothsayer, a madman, a poet, or a fabricator—because the inability of humans to produce its like insists that it must be the speech of Allah, the Glorified; however, the former explanation is more apparent.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **أَمْ خُلِقُوا مِنْ غَيْرِ شَيْءٍ أَمْ هُمُ الْخَالِقُونَ** **"Were they created from nothing? Or are they the creators?"** (52:35). The word **شَيْءٍ** (thing) is indefinite, implying an attribute suitable to the context; the estimation being: from nothing other than that from which other humans were created.

The meaning is: Rather, were these deniers created from something other than that from which other humans were created, such that it was appropriate to send a messenger, call to the truth, and be characterized by servitude (العبودية) to Him, the Exalted? Thus, no obligation (تكليف) would attach to them, nor would any command or prohibition be directed toward them, nor would their deeds be followed by reward or punishment, because they were created from something other than what others were created from.

There are other opinions regarding the meaning of this sentence.

It was said: The meaning is, were they brought into existence and proportioned with this exquisite proportion without a proportioner and creator? Thus, they have no need for a creator to manage their affairs.

It was said: The meaning is, were they created from something non-living? Thus, they are neither commanded nor prohibited, like inanimate objects.

It was said: The meaning is, were they created without a cause, or for no purpose of reward and punishment? Thus, for that reason, they do not listen.

It was said: The meaning is, were they created in vain (باطلاً), such that they are not held to account, nor commanded, nor prohibited?

The meaning we first presented is closer to the wording of the verse and more comprehensive.

And His saying: **أَمْ هُمُ الْخَالِقُونَ** **"Or are they the creators?"** (52:35). That is, creators of themselves? Thus, they would not be created by Allah, the Glorified, such that He would be their Lord and manage their affairs through command and prohibition.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **أَمْ خَلَقُوا السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ بَلْ لَا يُوقِنُونَ** **"Did they create the heavens and the earth? Rather, they have no certainty"** (52:36). That is, did they create the world such that they would be lords and gods, too exalted to be enslaved or tasked with the obligations of servitude? Rather, they are a people who have no certainty (يوقنون).

**His saying, the Exalted:** **أَمْ عِنْدَهُمْ خَزَائِنُ رَبِّكَ أَمْ هُمُ الْمُصَيْطِرُونَ** **"Do they possess the treasuries of your Lord? Or are they the controllers?"** (52:37). That is, rather, do they possess the treasuries of your Lord such that they may grant prophethood to whom they wish and withhold it from whom they wish, thereby denying you prophethood and messengership?

**And His saying:** **أَمْ هُمُ الْمُصَيْطِرُونَ** **"Or are they the controllers?"** (52:37). **السيطرة** (control)—and sometimes its *sin* is turned into a *sad*—means dominance and subjugation. The meaning is: Rather, are they the dominant ones, prevailing over Allah, the Glorified, such that they can strip from you what Allah has provided you of prophethood and messengership?

**His saying, the Exalted:** **أَمْ لَهُمْ سُلَّمٌ يَسْتَمِعُونَ فِيهِ فَلْيَأْتِ مُسْتَمِعُهُمْ بِسُلْطَانٍ مُبِينٍ** **"Do they have a ladder whereby they eavesdrop? Then let their eavesdropper bring a manifest proof"** (52:38). **السلم** is a ladder with rungs used as a means to ascend to high places. The word "listening" (الاستماع) here implies the meaning of ascending. **السلطان** means an argument or proof (البرهان).

The meaning is: Rather, do they have a ladder by which they ascend to the heaven, and by ascending it, they listen to the revelation, taking what is revealed to them and rejecting everything else? Then let their listener—meaning the one among them claiming to have listened—bring a manifest proof (حجة).

**His saying, the Exalted:** **أَمْ لَهُ الْبَنَاتُ وَلَكُمُ الْبَنُونَ** **"Does He have daughters while you have sons?"** (52:39). It was said: In this is a mockery of their intellects, as they attributed to Him, the Exalted, that which they themselves disdained.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **أَمْ تَسْأَلُهُمْ أَجْراً فَهُمْ مِنْ مَغْرَمٍ مُثْقَلُونَ** **"Do you ask them for a reward, so that they are overburdened by a debt?"** (52:40). Al-Raghib said: **الغرم** (debt/loss) is the damage that befalls a person in his wealth without any crime or betrayal on his part. **الإثقال** (overburdening) is to load with a heavy weight, which is a metaphor for hardship.

The meaning is: Rather, do you ask them for a reward for delivering your message, such that they find it difficult to bear the loss that befalls them by paying the reward?

**His saying, the Exalted:** **أَمْ عِنْدَهُمُ الْغَيْبُ فَهُمْ يَكْتُبُونَ** **"Do they possess [the knowledge of] the Unseen, so they write [it] down?"** (52:41). Some have mentioned that the Unseen (الغيب) refers to the Preserved Tablet (اللوح المحفوظ) in which the unseen matters are written. The meaning is: Rather, do they possess the Preserved Tablet, writing from it and informing the people of it? Thus, what they have informed about you would be from the Unseen about which there is no doubt.

It was said: The Unseen refers to the knowledge of the Unseen, and writing refers to establishing. The meaning is: Rather, do they possess the knowledge of the Unseen, so they establish what they have known as a law for the people, who must then obey them in what they have established? It was also said: **يكتبون** (they write) means they judge.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **أَمْ يُرِيدُونَ كَيْداً فَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا هُمُ الْمَكِيدُونَ** **"Do they seek to [concoct] a stratagem? But it is the faithless who are the victims of [their own] stratagem"** (52:42). **الكيد** (stratagem) is a type of trickery, as mentioned by al-Raghib. In *al-Majma‘*: **الكيد** is plotting (المكر), and it was said: it is doing that which necessitates rage in secret.

The apparent context is that what is meant by their stratagem is their plotting against the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) through what they charged him with—soothsaying, madness, poetry, and fabrication—so that people would turn away from him and distance themselves, thereby nullifying his call and extinguishing his light. This is a stratagem from them and a plot against their own souls, as they deprive them of eternal happiness and of embarking upon the straight path (صراط الحق) through that. Rather, it is a stratagem from Allah by cutting off success (التوفيق) from them and sealing their hearts.

It was said: The stratagem they intend is what occurred from them regarding him (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) in the Assembly Hall (دار الندوة), and the "faithless" (الذين كفروا) mentioned are the deniers, the companions of the Assembly Hall. Allah turned their stratagem against themselves and they were killed on the day of Badr. According to this, the speech is an informing of the Unseen, as the Surah was revealed long before that. This is far from the context.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **أَمْ لَهُمْ إِلَهٌ غَيْرُ اللَّهِ سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ** **"Do they have a god other than Allah? Immaculate is Allah of what they ascribe [to Him] as partners!"** (52:43). For if they had a god other than Allah, he would be their creator and the manager of their affairs; thus, they would be independent of Allah, the Glorified, and of responding to the call of His Messenger, and their god would help them and repel from them the punishment of Allah with which the deniers were promised and with which His Messenger warned them.

And His saying: **سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ** **"Immaculate is Allah of what they ascribe [to Him] as partners!"** (52:43) is a sanctification of Him, the Exalted, from having a partner as they claim. The **ما** in His saying **عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ** is infinitive (مصدرية); meaning: Glory be to Him from their shirk (شركهم).

**His saying, the Exalted:** **وَإِنْ يَرَوْا كِسْفاً مِنَ السَّمَاءِ سَاقِطاً يَقُولُوا سَحَابٌ مَرْكُومٌ** **"Even if they were to see a fragment of the sky falling down, they would say, ‘A mass of clouds!’"** (52:44). **الكسف** is a piece. **المركوم** is that which is piled up, one part falling upon another.

The meaning is: Their disbelief and their persistence in denying the true call has reached a point where, if they were to see a piece of the sky falling upon them, they would say it is a mass of clouds, having nothing to do with the sign of punishment. This is like His saying: **وَلَوْ فَتَحْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ بَاباً مِنَ السَّمَاءِ فَظَلُّوا فِيهِ يَعْرُجُونَ لَقَالُوا إِنَّمَا سُكِّرَتْ أَبْصَارُنَا** **"Even if We open a gate to the sky for them, and they were to keep ascending through it, they would surely say, ‘Our eyes have been dazzled!’"** (15:14-15).
